page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-180 is a high-density server instance powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 processors. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it features 180 virtual CPUs mapped to 90 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 720 GB of RAM, providing a balanced memory-to-core ratio of 4.0 GB per vCPU. This instance does not include local NVMe storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage for persistent data.

Benchmark evaluations place this server in the top tier (top 10%) for multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and database operations. Specifically, the stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU score rank in the top 10%, supported by high-speed memory bandwidth exceeding 356 GB/sec for large block read operations. Single-core performance, measured via Geekbench and stress-ng, consistently ranks in the strong top 25% tier. The instance also demonstrates top-tier capabilities in software compilation, ray tracing, and Redis SET operations, which reached 211.43 million operations per second.

For machine learning workloads, the server achieves top-tier prompt processing speeds across small, medium, and large models, such as 328.01 tokens per second on a medium 7B model. Text generation speeds vary, with the medium 7B model reaching a top-tier 52.74 tokens per second, while the small 135M model performs in the average tier. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512, AVX512_VNNI, and AVX512_BF16, which accelerate vector and neural network computations.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c3d-standard-180 offers high resource density and top-tier multi-threaded performance, making it suitable for large-scale parallel processing without the premium cost of GPU accelerators. However, the lack of local storage and dedicated GPUs represents a clear tradeoff for workloads requiring low-latency local disk I/O or hardware-accelerated deep learning. This configuration is optimized for CPU-bound workloads, large-scale software compilation, database operations, and text-based LLM inference.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-180 is an x86_64 server instance featuring 180 vCPUs across 90 physical AMD EPYC 9B14 cores and 720 GB of RAM. Lacking local storage and GPUs, it relies on network-attached storage. Benchmarks place its multi-core CPU performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations in the top 10% tier. It delivers strong single-core performance and top-tier LLM prompt processing speeds, supported by AVX-512 instruction sets. This instance is designed for CPU-intensive workloads, software compilation, and database operations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-standard-180 is an x86_64 server with 180 vCPUs, 720 GB RAM, and AMD EPYC processors, delivering top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for database and CPU-bound workloads.

tagline: A high-density, 180-vCPU AMD EPYC server delivering top-tier multi-core performance and memory bandwidth for intensive database and computational workloads.
bullet_points: 180 vCPUs and 720 GB RAM powered by AMD EPYC 9B14 processors
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth in the top 10%
Supports AVX-512 and AVX512_VNNI instruction sets for vector calculations
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in this configuration
Optimized for database operations, software compilation, and LLM inference
